items.title: Andes-virus cluster — MV Hondius cruise (South Atlantic), multi-country
items.summary: 8 confirmed and 3 probable/inconclusive Andes-virus cases on the MV Hondius, departed Ushuaia, Argentina on 2026-04-01 with stops in Antarctica, South Georgia Island, Tristan da Cunha, Saint Helena, Ascension Island. CDC met the ship at Las Palmas, Canary Islands on 2026-05-07. 3 deaths reported. Andes virus is the only hantavirus with documented person-to-person transmission.
items.citation: WHO Disease Outbreak News (2026-DON601). (2026). Andes-virus cluster — MV Hondius cruise (South Atlantic), multi-country. Retrieved 2026-05-15 from https://www.who.int/emergencies/disease-outbreak-news/item/2026-DON601.

items.title: ECDC — Communicable disease threats report, 9-13 May 2026, Week 20
items.summary: This issue of the ECDC Communicable Disease Threats Report (CDTR) covers the period 9-13 May 2026, and includes updates on avian influenza, human cases of swine influenza, mpox, and hantavirus.
items.citation: ECDC Communicable Disease Threats Report. (2026). ECDC — Communicable disease threats report, 9-13 May 2026, Week 20. Retrieved 2026-05-15 from https://www.ecdc.europa.eu/en/publications-data/communicable-disease-threats-report-9-13-may-2026-week-20.

items.title: WHO DON — Hantavirus cluster, cruise ship (2026-DON600)
items.summary: As of 8 May, a total of eight cases, including three deaths (case fatality ratio 38%), have been reported. Six cases have been laboratory-confirmed as hantavirus infections, with all identified as Andes virus (ANDV). Through the International Health Regulations (2005) (IHR) channel, National IHR Focal Points (NFPs) have all been informed and are supporting i
items.citation: WHO Disease Outbreak News (2026-DON600). (2026). WHO DON — Hantavirus cluster, cruise ship (2026-DON600). Retrieved 2026-05-15 from https://www.who.int/emergencies/disease-outbreak-news/item/2026-DON600.

items.title: Argentina — 102 confirmed HPS cases, 2025-2026 season (BEN SE-17/2026)
items.summary: Argentina BEN: 102 confirmed hantavirus cases for the 2025-2026 season through SE 17 of 2026 (frozen snapshot — live parse unavailable this run). Regional: Centro region 54% (Buenos Aires Province 43); NOA 36 cases (Salta 83%).
items.citation: Argentina BEN SE-17/2026. (2026). Argentina — 102 confirmed HPS cases, 2025-2026 season (BEN SE-17/2026). Retrieved 2026-05-15 from https://www.argentina.gob.ar/noticias/actualizacion-del-boletin-epidemiologico-nacional-de-la-semana-ndeg-17.

items.title: ECDC — Communicable disease threats report, 2-8 May 2026, Week 19
items.summary: This issue of the ECDC Communicable Disease Threats Report (CDTR) covers the period 2-8 May 2026, and includes updates on hantavirus, mpox, Middle East respiratory syndrome coronavirus (MERS-CoV), Salmonella Stanley, and an overview of respiratory virus epidemiology in the EU/EEA.
items.citation: ECDC Communicable Disease Threats Report. (2026). ECDC — Communicable disease threats report, 2-8 May 2026, Week 19. Retrieved 2026-05-15 from https://www.ecdc.europa.eu/en/publications-data/communicable-disease-threats-report-2-8-may-2026-week-19.

items.title: WHO DON — Hantavirus cluster, cruise ship (2026-DON599)
items.summary: As of 4 May 2026, seven cases (two laboratory confirmed cases of hantavirus and five suspected cases) have been identified, including three deaths, one critically ill patient and three individuals reporting mild symptoms. Illness onset occurred between 6 and 28 April 2026 and was characterized by fever, gastrointestinal symptoms, rapid progression to pneumon
